title: Port remaining Claude skills to Codex
description: Add Codex versions of the remaining Claude-only skills, share reusable scripts/reference docs where appropriate, and configure the Exa skill to use the Exa MCP tools.
close_reason: Ported remaining Claude skills to Codex, shared reusable resources, and verified Exa skill uses the configured Exa MCP server.

title: Add Exa MCP server to Codex config
description: Configure the Codex dotfiles config to register Exa as an MCP server using the official remote Exa MCP endpoint through mcp-remote, then validate and push the config change.
close_reason: Added Exa MCP server to Codex config and verified it appears in codex mcp list.

title: Add Codex handoff skill with shared script
description: Convert the Claude handoff skill for Codex and move the deterministic handoff-bd helper into a shared skill path with symlinks from both agent-specific skill directories.
close_reason: Added Codex handoff skill and moved handoff helper into shared symlinked path.

title: Initialize Codex beads integration
description: Why this issue exists: Codex should have first-class beads integration in this repo, matching the Claude setup, and Git should execute beads hooks instead of ignoring them. What needs to be done: refresh the Codex AGENTS.md integration, verify Claude integration remains installed, make the .beads hook path executable, and validate with beads setup checks and git hook status.
close_reason: Installed current Codex beads integration, fixed hook executability, and verified Git hook execution.

title: Unify global agent instructions
description: Why this issue exists: Claude and Codex should use one shared global instruction source. What was done: moved the shared instructions to agents/shared/instructions.md and made Dotbot link ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and ~/.codex/AGENTS.md directly to that file, without intermediate repo-level CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md files under agents/.

title: Update README for agents layout
description: Why this issue exists: the README needs to reflect the new tracked agents layout and explain the shared directory. What needs to be done: update README contents for the single install config, agents directory, Claude/Codex paths, and shared skill source material.
close_reason: Updated README for tracked agents layout and shared skill material

title: Port agent config into main dotfiles repo
description: Why this issue exists: the agent config should no longer live in a private submodule. What needs to be done: copy the current Claude skill/config files into the main repo under agents/, remove the claude-skills submodule registration, and update install/config docs for the no-submodule layout.
close_reason: Ported agent config into main repo and consolidated Dotbot install config

title: Parallel Claude worktree dispatcher skill
description:
A Claude skill (with associated scripts) that takes a set of issues, creates git worktrees for each group, writes task files, and launches parallel Claude sessions in Kitty tabs/windows. Replaces the manual process of: grouping bugs, creating worktrees, writing metaprompt files, and pasting `claude -p` into each terminal.

Should also handle creating a PR for each worktree, merging on github, and shuttling requested changes back and forth over GitHub PR comments.

## Requirements

- **Issue ingestion:** Accept issues from multiple sources -- a JSON/YAML file, inline args, piped stdin, or beads issues. Each issue needs at minimum a title and optional body/context. The script groups them (manually or by tag) into worktrees.
- **Worktree creation:** For each group, create a git worktree at an algorithmically named path under a gitignored dir in the repo, branch from current HEAD, and write a gitignored `WORKTREE_TASK.md` with the issues and a configurable metaprompt. Worktrees tagged with name of particular invocation of skill.
- **Task template:** WORKTREE_TASK.md should have standard sections (files to focus on, instructions, invariants, etc.) designed to make merging easy.
- **Kitty integration:** Use `kitty @ launch` or `kitten` to open a new tab/window per worktree, `cd` into it, and run `claude -p`. Tile them or use Kitty's tab bar so all sessions are visible. Name each tab after the worktree.
- **Metaprompt templating:** Configurable template (read task file, read CLAUDE.md, fix each bug, run tests, commit). Template lives in `~/.claude/` or repo's CLAUDE.md. Supports variables like `{test_command}`, `{task_file}`, etc.
- **Merge-back helper:** After sessions finish, command to merge each worktree branch back into main (or report status: which branches have commits, which are clean, which have conflicts). Optional: push as separate branches and use `gh` to make PRs for review.
- **Conflict avoidance:** When grouping issues, warn if two groups touch the same files (based on test file paths or source file mentions in issue text).
- **Beads integration (optional):** Make tasks from beads issues; prompt worktrees to open/close beads issues assigned to them. Specify beads issue IDs and the script figures out grouping. Properly clean up issues that have moved around. Issues that should be left open (worktree agents didn't fix properly) must stay open.

## Non-requirements

- No daemon, server, or web UI. Just a CLI script/skill.
- No automatic AI issue grouping. User specifies groups, or each issue gets its own worktree.
- No progress monitoring. User watches Kitty tabs.

## Hard rules

- NO centralized TODO files.
- WORKTREE_TASK.md files MUST be gitignored. Always. They cause merge conflict headaches otherwise.

## References

- Kitty remote control: https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/remote-control/
- git worktree docs: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-worktree
- Prior art: manual workflow documented in genomicsbench repo session history

title: AWS/GCP custom machine image provisioning script
description:
Script that spins up a custom machine image on AWS (and possibly a parallel script for GCP).

## Requirements

- Take args for disk size, RAM, CPUs, GPU, etc.
- Auto-provision SSH: generate/install SSH key on local machine, add entry to local SSH config.
- Subcommands:
  - Create machine
  - Take down machine
  - Resize resources
  - List machines
  - SSH into machine (via awscli)
- Optional: parallel implementation for GCP using gcloud.
